scope.summary: Implement MetaHuman DNA import into tailor_avatars using the Epic Games MetaHuman DNA C library (via FFI or a pre-baked mesh export path), storing the neutral-pose mesh and bone hierarchy so garments auto-fit MetaHuman proportions
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/avatars/import with format=metahuman_dna accepts a .dna file; if the MetaHuman DNA C library is not available at build time the endpoint returns HTTP 422 with UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT and documents the pre-baked mesh export fallback path (export from MetaHuman Creator as OBJ/glTF and use those import paths instead)

When the DNA library IS available (feature flag metahuman-dna-import), the neutral-pose mesh is extracted and stored in tailor_avatars with source_kind='metahuman' and measurements extracted via the standard 25-measurement pipeline (avatar body proxy path)
The 25 anthropometric measurements extracted match MetaHuman Creator's displayed values within 5mm for the reference character
The operator recipe documents both paths (native DNA and OBJ/glTF fallback) so operators without the DNA library are unblocked
scope.risk_if_missed: MetaHuman is the dominant digital human standard for UE5 productions. Without DNA import, every MetaHuman-based production pipeline must manually convert characters before fitting garments, introducing a manual step that competitors (MD 2025.2+) have eliminated.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
